text.title: CoT Production Rules and Gotchas
text.summary: Concrete rules for production CoT use, agentic loop costs, Extended Thinking caveats, and validation requirements.

rule.statement: For Claude, use `<thinking>` XML tags in the prompt to signal where the model should externalize reasoning. This is more reliable than free-form "think step by step" for structured outputs.
rule.rationale: Claude was fine-tuned to treat XML tag boundaries as semantic signals. The `<thinking>` / `<answer>` pattern produces cleaner separation between reasoning and output than untagged CoT.

rule.statement: In agentic loops, use CoT for the initial planning step only, then switch to direct prompts for execution steps. CoT token costs compound across turns.
rule.rationale: If an agent generates a CoT plan before each tool call in a 10-step pipeline, total token cost is 10x the single-call overhead. Plan once, execute directly.

rule.statement: Always validate the final answer independently for numerical or verifiable claims — do not trust the reasoning trace alone. Zero-shot CoT can produce confident-sounding wrong reasoning.
rule.rationale: CoT improves accuracy on average but not on every instance. The model may generate a plausible-looking incorrect chain. For math/code, run the answer through a verifier or test suite.

antipattern.description: Using Extended Thinking on every query regardless of complexity.
antipattern.reason: Extended Thinking tokens are billed at output token rates (~$0.30/call at 4K thinking budget with Opus). Multiply by query volume. Reserve for tasks where reasoning quality matters more than cost.

antipattern.description: Assuming thinking tokens are cached by Prompt Caching.
antipattern.reason: Prompt Caching applies to input tokens, not to Extended Thinking output. Thinking cost cannot be amortized across requests — it is paid fresh every call.

antipattern.description: Running Self-Consistency on high-stakes automated pipelines without logging disagreement rate.
antipattern.reason: If >40% of samples disagree, majority vote is unreliable. Escalate to human review rather than returning a 3-2 majority answer.
All samples will produce identical reasoning paths at temperature=0, defeating the purpose of sampling diverse paths. Use temperature=0.7.
